{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53984352?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kos0ng", "id": 53984352, "login": "kos0ng", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjUzOTg0MzUy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kos0ng", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2025-48370", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 2.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "### Impact
The library functions `getUserById`, `deleteUser`, `updateUserById`, `listFactors` and `deleteFactor` did not require the user supplied values to be valid UUIDs. This could lead to a URL path traversal, resulting in the wrong API function being called.

Implementations that follow security best practice and validate user controlled inputs, such as the `userId` are not affected by this.

### Patches
Strict value checks have been added to all affected functions. These functions now require that the `userId` and `factorId` parameters MUST be valid UUID (v4).

**Patched version:** >= 2.69.1

### Workarounds
Implementations that follow security best practice and validate user controlled inputs, such as the `userId` are not affected by this. It is recommended that users of the auth-js library always follow security best practice and validate all inputs, before passing these to other functions or libraries.

### References

title: auth-js Vulnerable to Insecure Path Routing from Malformed User Input
